label_reasons: Pinned NodeGoat test config hardcodes a ZAP API key used by security test automation.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded credential at line 6
findings.description: A credential, key, or password appears to be hardcoded at L6: `zapApiKey: "v9dn0balpqas1pcc281tn5ood1",`. Commit history will permanently expose this credential even after removal.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets to environment variables: `process.env.API_KEY`. Use a secrets manager for production.

rule.title: Hardcoded credential
rule.summary: A credential, key, or password appears to be hardcoded.
rule.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables: `process.env.API_KEY`. Use a secrets manager for production.

label_reasons: Pinned Django GDAL raster clone helper composes a virtual filesystem clone path with os.path.join() on dynamic input inside clone().

findings.title: Excessive complexity in __init__() (CC=26)
findings.description: `__init__()` at L77 has cyclomatic complexity 26. Functions above 15 are hard to test, debug, and maintain.
findings.suggestion: Extract sub-functions for distinct logic branches or use lookup tables.

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in clone() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `clone()` passes `VSI_MEM_FILESYSTEM_BASE_PATH` (dynamic/path-like input) to `os.path.join()` at L470 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

label_reasons: Pinned NodeGoat profile route contains an active catastrophic-backtracking regex example.

findings.title: CWE-1333: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex at line 59
findings.description: Regex with nested quantifiers or ambiguous alternation at L59: `const regexPattern = /([0-9]+)+\#/;`.
findings.suggestion: Use safe regex patterns or a non-backtracking engine.

rule.title: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex
rule.summary: Regex with nested quantifiers or ambiguous alternation.
rule.remediation: Use safe regex patterns or a non-backtracking engine.

label_reasons: Pinned NodeGoat shared config hardcodes cookie and crypto secrets instead of sourcing them from environment or a secret store.

findings.title: CWE-798: Hardcoded credential at line 8
findings.description: A credential, key, or password appears to be hardcoded at L8: `cookieSecret: "session_cookie_secret_key_here",`. Commit history will permanently expose this credential even after removal.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets to environment variables: `process.env.API_KEY`. Use a secrets manager for production.

rule.title: Hardcoded credential
rule.summary: A credential, key, or password appears to be hardcoded.
rule.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables: `process.env.API_KEY`. Use a secrets manager for production.

label_reasons: Pinned NodeGoat contribution route contains multiple direct `eval()` code-execution hits.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via eval() at line 32
findings.description: `eval()` is called with dynamic content at L32: `eval(req.body.preTax)`. If the argument is attacker-influenced, arbitrary JavaScript can execute.
findings.suggestion: Remove `eval()`. Parse data with `JSON.parse()` or use a safe expression interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via eval()
rule.summary: `eval()` called.
rule.remediation: Eliminate eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression library.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via eval() at line 33
findings.description: `eval()` is called with dynamic content at L33: `eval(req.body.afterTax)`. If the argument is attacker-influenced, arbitrary JavaScript can execute.
findings.suggestion: Remove `eval()`. Parse data with `JSON.parse()` or use a safe expression interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via eval()
rule.summary: `eval()` called.
rule.remediation: Eliminate eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression library.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via eval() at line 34
findings.description: `eval()` is called with dynamic content at L34: `eval(req.body.roth)`. If the argument is attacker-influenced, arbitrary JavaScript can execute.
findings.suggestion: Remove `eval()`. Parse data with `JSON.parse()` or use a safe expression interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via eval()
rule.summary: `eval()` called.
rule.remediation: Eliminate eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression library.

label_reasons: Pinned NodeGoat login route has no visible brute-force or per-IP rate limiting.
Pinned NodeGoat signup route is also exposed without visible registration throttling.
Pinned NodeGoat learn-link route redirects directly from a query-string URL.

findings.title: CWE-307: No rate limiting on authentication route at line 34
findings.description: Authentication route `/login` at L34 (`app.post("/login", sessionHandler.handleLoginRequest)`) has no rate-limiting middleware in scope. An attacker can brute-force credentials, OTPs, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Apply rate limiting, for example `const limiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 10 })`, or attach a route-scoped/per-prefix limiter before the auth handler.

rule.title: JS auth route missing rate limiting
rule.summary: Flags login-like routes with no obvious rate-limiter middleware in scope.
rule.remediation: Apply a per-IP or per-account rate limiter before the auth handler.

findings.title: CWE-352: State-changing route may lack CSRF protection at line 34
findings.description: POST/PUT/DELETE route defined at L34 without detectable CSRF token validation. Cross-site request forgery can force authenticated users to perform unintended actions.
findings.suggestion: Use csurf middleware or implement CSRF token validation for all mutating routes.

rule.title: CWE-352: State-changing route may lack CSRF protection at line 34
rule.summary: Flags browser-exposed state mutations that appear to lack CSRF defenses.
rule.remediation: Require CSRF tokens or same-site protections for cookie-authenticated browser flows.

findings.title: CWE-307: No rate limiting on registration route at line 38
findings.description: Registration route `/signup` at L38 (`app.post("/signup", sessionHandler.handleSignup)`) has no rate-limiting middleware in scope. An attacker can brute-force credentials, OTPs, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Apply rate limiting, for example `const limiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 10 })`, or attach a route-scoped/per-prefix limiter before the auth handler.

rule.title: JS auth route missing rate limiting
rule.summary: Flags login-like routes with no obvious rate-limiter middleware in scope.
rule.remediation: Apply a per-IP or per-account rate limiter before the auth handler.

findings.description: POST/PUT/DELETE route defined at L38 without detectable CSRF token validation. Cross-site request forgery can force authenticated users to perform unintended actions.
findings.suggestion: Use csurf middleware or implement CSRF token validation for all mutating routes.

rule.title: CWE-352: State-changing route may lack CSRF protection at line 38
rule.summary: Flags browser-exposed state mutations that appear to lack CSRF defenses.
rule.remediation: Require CSRF tokens or same-site protections for cookie-authenticated browser flows.

findings.title: CWE-601: Open redirect via tainted variable at line 72
findings.description: `res.redirect()` at L72 uses a user-influenced destination: `res.redirect(req.query.url)`. Attackers can redirect victims to untrusted sites.
findings.suggestion: Validate redirect targets against an allowlist of permitted relative paths or trusted hosts.

rule.title: JS open redirect via tainted target
rule.summary: Flags redirects or redirect helpers driven by user-controlled targets.
rule.remediation: Restrict redirect targets to relative paths or explicit trusted hosts.

findings.title: CWE-352: State-mutating route may lack CSRF protection at line 34
findings.description: POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE route defined at L34 (`app.post("/login", sessionHandler.handleLoginRequest);`). Without CSRF protection, authenticated users can be tricked into submitting unwanted requests.
findings.suggestion: Use CSRF middleware or set `SameSite=Strict` on session cookies.

rule.title: State-mutating route may lack CSRF protection
rule.summary: POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE route defined (matched code).
rule.remediation: Use CSRF middleware or set `SameSite=Strict` on session cookies.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via new Function() at line 2
findings.description: `new Function(...)` at L2: `!function(n){"function"==typeof define&&define.amd?define(["jquery"],n):"object"==typeof m`. This is equivalent to eval(); any user-controlled string becomes executable code. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low)
findings.suggestion: Avoid new Function(). If dynamic logic is required, use a safe interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via new Function()
rule.summary: `new Function(...)`.
rule.remediation: Avoid new Function(). If dynamic logic is required, use a safe interpreter.

findings.title: CWE-98: Dynamic require() with variable path at line 2
findings.description: `require()` called with a non-literal argument at L2: `!function(n){"function"==typeof define&&define.amd?define(["jquery"],n):"object"==typeof m`. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low)
findings.suggestion: Use static `require('module-name')` only. Never pass user input to require().

rule.title: Dynamic require() with variable path
rule.summary: `require()` called with a non-literal argument.
rule.remediation: Use static `require('module-name')` only. Never pass user input to require().

findings.title: CWE-338: Weak PRNG (Math.random) in security context at line 2
findings.description: `Math.random()` used near security-sensitive code at L2: `!function(n){"function"==typeof define&&define.amd?define(["jquery"],n):"object"==typeof m`. Math.random is NOT cryptographically secure. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low)
findings.suggestion: Use `crypto.randomBytes(32)` (Node.js) or `crypto.getRandomValues()` (browser).

rule.title: Weak PRNG (Math.random) in security context
rule.summary: `Math.random()` used near security-sensitive code.
rule.remediation: Use `crypto.randomBytes(32)` (Node.js) or `crypto.getRandomValues()` (browser).

findings.title: CWE-1333: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex at line 2
findings.description: Regex with nested quantifiers or ambiguous alternation at L2: `!function(n){"function"==typeof define&&define.amd?define(["jquery"],n):"object"==typeof m`. (minified bundle without source map; confidence downgraded to low) (vendor/minified asset heuristic downgraded) (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Use safe regex patterns or a non-backtracking engine.

rule.title: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex
rule.summary: Regex with nested quantifiers or ambiguous alternation.
rule.remediation: Use safe regex patterns or a non-backtracking engine.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via innerHTML assignment at line 217
findings.description: Unsanitized data assigned to `innerHTML` or `outerHTML` at L217: `wrapper1.innerHTML = ``. An attacker can inject a `<script>` tag or event handler that executes in the victim's browser.
findings.suggestion: Use `textContent` instead of `innerHTML`, or sanitize with DOMPurify.sanitize(). Never concatenate user data into HTML.

rule.title: XSS via innerHTML assignment
rule.summary: Unsanitized data assigned to `innerHTML` or `outerHTML`.
rule.remediation: Use `textContent` instead of `innerHTML`, or sanitize with DOMPurify.sanitize(). Never concatenate user data into HTML.
known_limitations: DOM and template heuristics are strongest when flows remain inside a file or helper chain.

findings.title: CWE-79: XSS via innerHTML assignment at line 229
findings.description: Unsanitized data assigned to `innerHTML` or `outerHTML` at L229: `wrapper2.innerHTML = ``. An attacker can inject a `<script>` tag or event handler that executes in the victim's browser.
findings.suggestion: Use `textContent` instead of `innerHTML`, or sanitize with DOMPurify.sanitize(). Never concatenate user data into HTML.

rule.title: XSS via innerHTML assignment
rule.summary: Unsanitized data assigned to `innerHTML` or `outerHTML`.
rule.remediation: Use `textContent` instead of `innerHTML`, or sanitize with DOMPurify.sanitize(). Never concatenate user data into HTML.
known_limitations: DOM and template heuristics are strongest when flows remain inside a file or helper chain.

findings.description: Object modification using `__proto__`, `constructor.prototype`, or `Object.assign` with unchecked user input at L519: `BaseConstructor.prototype = SuperClass.prototype;`. (vendor/minified asset heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Validate keys, strip `__proto__` / `constructor`, and prefer safe merge patterns.

findings.title: CWE-1333: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex at line 5480
findings.description: Regex with nested quantifiers or ambiguous alternation at L5480: `var WIDTH = /^width:(([-+]?([0-9]*\.)?[0-9]+)(px|em|ex|%|in|cm|mm|pt|pc))/i;`. (vendor/minified asset heuristic downgraded) (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Use safe regex patterns or a non-backtracking engine.

rule.title: Potential ReDoS — catastrophic backtracking regex

findings.title: CWE-601: Open redirect in login() via redirect()
findings.description: `login()` passes user-controlled `redirect_url` to `redirect()` at L431. An attacker can craft a URL that redirects victims to a malicious site for phishing or credential theft.
findings.suggestion: Validate redirect targets against an allowlist of safe paths/domains. Use `url_for()` for internal redirects. Check with `url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme(url, allowed_hosts)`.

rule.title: Python open redirect via redirect()
rule.summary: Flags `redirect()` calls that forward user-controlled URLs without allowlist validation.
rule.remediation: Use `url_for()` for internal redirects or allowlist external destinations explicitly.

findings.title: Implicit None return in link_file() — can fall off end
findings.description: `link_file()` has 1 branches that return a value and 1 that return None implicitly. Callers that do not check for None will encounter AttributeError or incorrect logic.
findings.suggestion: Ensure all code paths return an explicit value, or annotate the return type.

rule.title: Inconsistent return paths causing implicit None
rule.summary: Flags functions whose branches mix explicit return values with implicit `None` fall-through.
rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.

findings.title: Implicit None return in copy_file() — can fall off end
findings.description: `copy_file()` has 1 branches that return a value and 1 that return None implicitly. Callers that do not check for None will encounter AttributeError or incorrect logic.

rule.title: Inconsistent return paths causing implicit None

rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.

rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: CWE-200: Traceback/error details exposed to users at line 1007
findings.description: `traceback.print_exc()` or equivalent at L1007 may leak internal paths, versions, and logic to end users.
findings.suggestion: Use `logger.exception()` server-side and return a generic error message to clients.

rule.title: Stack trace or internal error details exposed to users
rule.summary: Flags `traceback.print_exc()`, `sys.exc_info()` in response bodies, or debug error handlers leaking internals.
rule.remediation: Use generic error handlers in production; log details with `logger.exception()` server-side only.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in auto_find_instance_path() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `auto_find_instance_path()` passes `package_path` (dynamic/path-like input) to `os.path.join()` at L517 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: Silent exception swallowing in parse_http_date_safe()
findings.description: `parse_http_date_safe()` catches Exception with `pass` at L146, hiding disk I/O failures, permission errors, data corruption, and any other exception. (framework-internal implementation heuristic downgraded)
findings.suggestion: Log and re-raise: `logger.exception('Unexpected error'); raise`
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: except Exception:
AFTER:      logger.exception("Unexpected error")
            raise

rule.title: Silent exception swallowing
rule.summary: Flags broad exception handlers that swallow failures with `pass` or `continue`.
rule.remediation: Log and re-raise unexpected errors or catch only specific exceptions you can safely handle.

findings.title: Implicit None return in from_pgraster() — can fall off end
findings.description: `from_pgraster()` has 1 branches that return a value and 1 that return None implicitly. Callers that do not check for None will encounter AttributeError or incorrect logic.
findings.suggestion: Ensure all code paths return an explicit value, or annotate the return type.

rule.title: Inconsistent return paths causing implicit None
rule.summary: Flags functions whose branches mix explicit return values with implicit `None` fall-through.
rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.

findings.title: Excessive complexity in templatize() (CC=52)
findings.description: `templatize()` at L39 has cyclomatic complexity 52. Functions above 15 are hard to test, debug, and maintain.
findings.suggestion: Extract sub-functions for distinct logic branches or use lookup tables.

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.
